title: Krea2: test a speed-up LoRA on RAW vs the Turbo transformer — collapse 4 transformers to 2 if results match
description:
GOAL: find out whether Krea2 RAW + a speed-up (distill) LoRA produces results comparable to the separately-shipped Turbo transformer. If yes, drop the Turbo weights entirely and move Krea2 onto the runtime tier-radio model already proven on Qwen-Edit 2511 (ONE card, tier picked at runtime) instead of the current 4-card transformer set.

THIS IS A TEST-FIRST CARD. Nothing ships until the A/B says the quality holds. If it does not, the card dies and the 4-transformer layout stays — that is an acceptable outcome, not a failure.

WHY IT IS WORTH TESTING (the prize, measured):
The 4 Krea2 transformers in js/data/modelConstants/modelDeps.js are:
- krea2-turbo-transformer          krea2_turbo_fp8_scaled.safetensors          12.24GB
- krea2-turbo-transformer-nsfw     lustify-v10-krea-turbo-int8_convrot         12.25GB
- krea2-raw-transformer            krea2_raw_int8_convrot                      13.49GB
- krea2-raw-transformer-nsfw       lustify-v10-krea-raw-int8_convrot           13.15GB
Dropping the two Turbo weights = ~24.5GB less to host on R2, download, and store per user, replaced by one small LoRA. Same shape as the Qwen-Edit win: ONE ModelDef + a runtime tier radio, NOT N cards over the same family (see memory project_qwen_edit_shape_and_limits).

WHAT TO TEST (the actual question):
RAW + speed LoRA at low steps vs Turbo at its baked settings. Same prompt, same seed, same resolution, both SFW and NSFW weights. Judge: subject fidelity, hands, skin texture, prompt adherence, and wall-clock. Krea2 already has a documented sampler baseline to compare against — docs/models/krea2/samplers.md (euler-beta @ 40 wins on RAW; the earlier 83s-vs-55s gap was the prompt ENHANCER, not the sampler). Reuse that harness rather than inventing a new one.

TIER/SAMPLER FACTS THIS MUST RESPECT (verified in the baked runtime files 2026-07-19):
Every krea2_t2i_*.json carries THREE ClownsharKSampler_Beta nodes and Input_Tier selects which runs:
- node 162 — cfg 1, 3 steps    (distilled/turbo path)
- node 72  — cfg 1, 6 steps    (distilled/turbo path)
- node 311 — cfg 2.5, 25 steps (RAW/guided path)
So cfg is the real dividing line, not the file name.

NEGATIVE-PROMPT GATING — CARRY THIS INTO THE DESIGN (user-flagged):
Input_Negative (node 283) feeds CLIPTextEncode (282) + Krea2EditGroundedEncode (299) in ALL variants. Today it is gated STRUCTURALLY, not by a switch: on the cfg-1 tiers classifier-free guidance is inactive, so the negative conditioning is computed and discarded — harmless, no bug, nothing currently mis-wired. If the tiers collapse into ONE card with a runtime radio, that free structural gating DISAPPEARS: a single graph would have to decide per-tier whether the negative does anything. Decide deliberately at design time — either keep the negative UI visible only on the guided tier, or accept a dead control on the fast tiers. Do not let it become an invisible no-op the user can type into.

ALSO IN SCOPE IF IT SHIPS:
- The identity-edit path is the reason RAW exists at all (MPI-282: 'Raw has a WORKING cfg, so it drives the identity-edit LoRA; Balanced/Turbo at cfg 1 starves the edit conditioning'). A speed LoRA on RAW must NOT break edit — test edit as well as t2i, or explicitly scope edit to the guided tier.
- Shared-dep guard: removing two transformers touches install/uninstall accounting. Read the MPI-276 invariants before editing either guard (memory project_shared_dep_guard_exclusive_evidence).
- Style LoRAs (styleLoraImages) and the Krea2 edit LoRA are known NOT to compose (MPI-282). A speed LoRA is a third stack member — check it composes with both.

OPEN QUESTION: which speed-up LoRA. Nothing is pinned yet; sourcing it is step 1.

ACCEPTANCE:
- A/B run exists: RAW+speed-LoRA vs Turbo, same prompt/seed/resolution, SFW and NSFW, with images and wall-clock recorded in the card's research/
- A written verdict on whether quality holds — including the case for KILLING the card if it does not
- If it proceeds: negative-prompt behaviour per tier is an explicit, documented decision, not an accident of cfg
- If it proceeds: identity-edit still works (or edit is deliberately scoped to the guided tier)

READ FIRST: docs/models/krea2/samplers.md (A/B harness + the enhancer-not-sampler lesson), docs/models/krea2/injection.md, js/data/modelConstants/modelDeps.js (the 4 transformer entries). Related cards: MPI-282 (RAW exists for edit cfg), MPI-300 (the tier-radio shape to copy), MPI-276 (shared-dep guard invariants).
